title: Optimized ERC721 transferFrom with storage hitchhiking, XOR owner swap, and dual-error packed revert
trigger_patterns: NFT ownership transfer with approval check
explanation: Seven techniques: (1) ownership slot derived as add(id, add(id, keccak256())) exploits EVM storage layout so adjacent token IDs have adjacent slots, (2) XOR swap replaces the owner address bits without touching upper 96 extraData bits — no masking needed, (3) two different error selectors packed in one uint256, offset by condition using shl(2, iszero(owner)) — used for both ownership and balance checks, (4) log4(codesize(), 0x00) uses codesize as a valid but irrelevant pointer for zero-length data, (5) approved address stored at add(1, ownershipSlot) for free — no keccak256 needed, (6) approval check uses isApprovedForAll via keccak256(0x0c, 0x30) with the master slot seed already in memory, (7) NotOwnerNorApproved uses a separate 4-byte custom error selector 0x4b6e7f18.
when_to_apply: ERC721 transferFrom where ownership and extra data are packed in same slot
when_not_to_apply: Contracts where extraData bits must survive independent of owner, ERC721A style consecutive minting
